principles: Confidence comes from clarity and action.
Master the mind and the body follows.
Control what you can control and release what you cannot.
Pressure is a privilege — it means the moment matters.
Your next action is always within your control.

three_rs: Reset: acknowledge emotion and release the previous moment.
Regroup: restore clarity and identify controllables.
Refocus: commit attention to the next action.
cognitive_appraisal: Interpretation: what you make it mean.
Emotion: what you feel as a result.

self_talk_system.label: I notice the feeling.
self_talk_system.reframe: This is my body preparing to perform.

examples.label: I notice the frustration after that misplaced pass.
examples.reframe: One pass does not define my performance.
examples.redirect: Win the next ball. Play simple.

examples.label: I notice the nerves before kick-off.
examples.reframe: This is my body preparing to compete at a high level.
examples.redirect: Focus on my first touch and first action.

examples.label: I notice the anger after that tackle.
examples.reframe: Anger is energy. I choose where it goes.
examples.redirect: Channel it into the next defensive action.

examples.label: I notice doubt creeping in after that miss.
examples.reframe: Doubt is a thought, not a fact. My preparation is real.
examples.redirect: Commit fully to the next involvement.

examples.label: I notice embarrassment after that error.
examples.reframe: Every player makes errors. Recovery speed defines elite performers.
examples.redirect: Reset body language. Next action, full commitment.

reset_techniques: Physical release: shake hands, roll shoulders, unclench jaw.
Verbal cue: say 'release' or 'next' under your breath.
Body language reset: stand tall, chest open, eyes up.
regroup_techniques: Controllables check: what can I influence right now?
Role clarity: what does the team need from me in this moment?
Simplify: reduce to one clear task.
Composure anchor: touch your wristband or boot as a cue to regroup.
refocus_techniques: Next-action targeting: name one specific thing you will do next.
Attentional narrowing: where is the ball, where is my marker?
Process commitment: focus on execution, not outcome.
Visualise success: see yourself completing the next action cleanly.
